title: cleistanthus acuminatus
intro: Cleistanthus acuminatus, also known as the bitter cleistanthus or bitter wood, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Cleistanthus acuminatus include reducing fever, treating malaria, and alleviating various infections, making it a valuable remedy in folk medicine. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are typically dried, powdered, and used to make teas or taken as capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
